title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Patriots Charitable Foundation.
Despite a Stage 3 breast cancer diagnosis in 2023, McHugh returned to running after five months of treatment.
Inspired by her mentor Susan Hurley, McHugh is running to honor friends, family, and her resilience against cancer.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey questioned Meta's plans to add facial recognition to smart glasses, citing privacy concerns.
Markey, joined by two other senators, warned the tech could be used to monitor protesters without consent.
Meta has until April 6 to clarify data collection and privacy safeguards for facial recognition in smart glasses.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy's city council delayed a vote on a $2.65 million plan to replace PFAS-contaminated firefighter gear.
Firefighters expressed frustration over the delay, citing health risks from PFAS exposure in current gear.
Some councilors seek more time to assess cost and durability before approving PFAS-free turnout gear funding.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro officials propose converting Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to address $57 million in needed repairs.
The transition would focus on native wildlife, reducing long-term costs and possibly offering free or reduced admission.
Zoo officials focus on animal care during discussions, while some critics argue the plan could lead to the zoo's closure.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: A blizzard forced Watertown couple Abigail Brown and Colin Wong to hold a last-minute microwedding on January 26, 2026.
FX's 'Love Story' prompts nostalgia for '90s communication, showcasing John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette Kennedy's romance.
Discussion raised on the evolving use of 'partner' for relationships, questioning if it's interchangeable with 'boyfriend'.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, 44, of Burlington, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH.
Averback's father died of glioblastoma in 2020; he is a testicular cancer survivor.
Despite a serious blood infection, Averback recovered and continued training for the marathon.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots targeted Eagles WR A.J. Brown in trade talks, but offer was declined by Philadelphia.
Eagles reportedly waiting until June 1 to trade Brown to save salary cap space.
Photos show A.J. Brown working out with Patriots safety Kevin Byard, fueling trade speculations.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens completed his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
The Boston Bruins are considering signing Hagens to an entry-level contract after his season-end.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney discussed openness to adding Hagens, leveraging his speed and playmaking abilities.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' with Cillian Murphy streams on Netflix, set in 1940s Birmingham during WWII.
'Lone Wolf McQuade' starring Chuck Norris, available on Tubi and Prime Video, highlights Norris's role as a Texas Ranger.
Bradley Cooper directs Will Arnett in 'Is This Thing On?' about a stand-up comic, streaming on Disney+.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora enters his eighth season as Red Sox manager and is under contract through 2027.
Cora has expressed contentment with the team's current state but remains non-committal about his future after 2027.
Cora could surpass Terry Francona in all-time managerial wins for the Red Sox, requiring 134 more victories.
